description_long: This temple features a prominent central shikhara (spire) with a flag at its peak, surrounded by smaller domes and ornate pillars. The structure is richly decorated with sculpted reliefs and intricate carvings, especially around the base and columns. The main entrance is marked by a series of arched gateways supported by detailed columns, leading into a spacious mandapa (hall) area. The roofline is characterized by a combination of stepped domes and a towering pyramidal spire, typical of North Indian temple architecture. The exterior is predominantly a reddish-brown stone, with lighter accents highlighting architectural details. The temple sits on a raised platform, with steps and open walkways providing access around the perimeter.
description: A richly ornamented temple with a tall central spire, multiple domes, and intricately carved columns and arches, all in reddish-brown stone.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with three large arches, ornate columns, and the central shikhara rising behind.
description_view_1: Direct axial view highlighting the alignment of the domes and the central spire with a flag at the top.
description_view_2: Side view revealing the layered domes, detailed column work, and the temple's raised platform.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the central shikhara and the symmetrical arrangement of smaller domes.
attribute_ornamentation: dense sculptural reliefs, geometric and floral motifs, decorative finials, and column carvings
attribute_entrance_design: Triple-arched gateway framed by intricately carved columns and lintels, with steps leading up to the main hall and ornamental detailing above the arches.
